name: Vector Computation
desc: NumPy vectorization and Pandas selection — leave Python loops behind for compiled array math.

questions.card: A NumPy array is one block where math hits every element at once.
questions.mission: Pack the probe speeds 88, 132, 99, 145 into speeds.

questions.card: Array methods reduce the whole vector in compiled code.
questions.mission: Store the mean of speeds in avg.

questions.card: A DataFrame gives records named columns and an index.
questions.mission: Turn probes into a DataFrame df.

questions.card: A comparison yields a True/False mask; index the frame with it.
questions.mission: Keep probes faster than 100 into fast.
Min-max scale speeds into norm.

questions.card: .std() is the standard deviation.
questions.mission: Store the std of speeds in sd.

questions.card: .sum() reduces to one number.
questions.mission: Sum speeds into total.

questions.card: .argmax() returns the index of the max.
questions.mission: Index of the fastest probe into peak.

questions.card: np.sort returns a sorted copy.
questions.mission: Sort speeds ascending into ranked.
Count probes with speed > 100 into n (vectorized).
Make 0..9 into r.
Five zeros into z.

questions.card: .reshape(r, c) changes the view, not the data.
questions.mission: Reshape 0..5 into a 2×3 grid grid.
Build a float array of 1,2,3 into arr.
Double every element of arr into scaled.
Add 10 to every element of arr into shifted.
Take the first column of grid into col.

questions.card: Index with a mask to pull matching elements.
questions.mission: Keep only the positives of arr into pos.

questions.card: np.where(cond, a, b) selects per element.
questions.mission: Map arr to +1 / −1 by sign into signs.
Dot a with b into d.
Column sums of m into col_sums.
Row means of m into row_means.
Roots of arr into roots.
Exp of arr into e.

questions.card: np.clip(arr, lo, hi) bounds values.
questions.mission: Clip arr to [0, 1] into c.

questions.card: np.cumsum is the prefix sum.
questions.mission: Cumulative sum of arr into cs.
Index of the max of arr into i.
Distinct values of arr into u.
Join a and b into both.

questions.card: np.linspace(a, b, n) — n points inclusive.
questions.mission: Five points from 0 to 1 into xs.
Grab the speed column into s.

questions.card: .loc[label] selects by index label.
questions.mission: Row at index 0 into row.

questions.card: .iloc[r, c] selects by position.
questions.mission: Value at row 0, column 1 into cell.
Rows where signal < 80 into weak.

questions.card: Assign a computed Series to a new column.
questions.mission: Add a boolean column ok = speed > 100.
Drop signal into df2.
Rename speed → v into df3.
Tally the probe column into counts.
Sort rows by speed into ranked.

questions.card: .head(n) peeks at the top.
questions.mission: First 3 rows into top.
Mean speed per band into g.
Mean and max of speed into stats.
Fill NaNs with 0 into filled.
Drop rows with NaN into clean.
Add a km column = speed × 1.6.
Mean of the speed column into mspeed.

questions.card: .nlargest(n, col) — the n biggest.
questions.mission: Two fastest rows into top2.
Describe the speed column into summary.

questions.card: .reset_index() turns the index back into a column.
questions.mission: Flatten the grouped result g into flat.

questions.card: pd.merge(a, b, on=key) — a SQL join.
questions.mission: Join df with meta on probe into joined.

questions.card: pyplot is the Matlab-style API to matplotlib.
questions.mission: Import matplotlib.pyplot as plt.

questions.card: plt.plot(x, y) — the default line chart.
questions.mission: Plot xs, ys.

questions.card: plt.scatter(x, y) — one dot per pair.
questions.mission: Scatter height vs weight.

questions.card: plt.hist(x, bins=n) reveals distribution shape.
questions.mission: Draw ages with 20 bins.
Set title to Speed.

questions.card: sns.heatmap for correlations / confusion matrices.
questions.mission: Heatmap corr.
Box speed.
Save to out.png.

questions.card: plt.subplots(r, c) makes a grid of axes.
questions.mission: Make 1 row, 2 cols into fig, axes.

questions.card: plotly.express is the fast, interactive path.
questions.mission: Import as px.
Median of x into med.
Variance into v.
Std into s.

questions.card: np.quantile — value at the p-th cutoff.
questions.mission: Get p95 into q95.
Correlate a, b into r.
Compute into z.
Compare a, b into t, p.

questions.card: np.random.normal(mu, sigma, n) — draw from Gaussian.
questions.mission: Draw 100 with mean 0, std 1 into x.
Half-width: hw = 1.96 * s / n**0.5.
Fit into reg.

questions.card: SELECT ... FROM t — read every column.
questions.mission: Read all rows from users into q.
Users with age > 18 as q.

questions.card: GROUP BY buckets rows for aggregation.
questions.mission: Count users per country into q.
questions.ph: q = 'SELECT country, COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY country'
questions.sol: q = 'SELECT country, COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY country'
questions.re: ^q\s*=\s*['"]SELECT country, COUNT\(\*\) FROM users GROUP BY country['"]$

questions.card: ORDER BY col DESC sorts.
questions.mission: Top 5 by score descending.
questions.ph: q = 'SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 5'
questions.sol: q = 'SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 5'
questions.re: ^q\s*=\s*['"]SELECT \* FROM users ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 5['"]$

questions.card: INNER JOIN keeps matching rows from both tables.
questions.mission: Join orders o to users u ON o.uid = u.id.
questions.ph: q = 'SELECT * FROM orders o INNER JOIN users u ON o.uid = u.id'
questions.sol: q = 'SELECT * FROM orders o INNER JOIN users u ON o.uid = u.id'
questions.re: ^q\s*=\s*['"]SELECT \* FROM orders o INNER JOIN users u ON o\.uid = u\.id['"]$

questions.card: pd.read_sql executes a query into a DataFrame.
questions.mission: Run q over conn into df.

questions.card: DDL — CREATE TABLE declares the schema.
questions.mission: Two cols: id INT, name TEXT.
questions.ph: q = 'CREATE TABLE users (id INT, name TEXT)'
questions.sol: q = 'CREATE TABLE users (id INT, name TEXT)'

questions.mission: Insert one row (1, 'ada').
Open app.db as conn.

questions.card: Window: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ...).
questions.mission: Rank within each uid by ts.
questions.ph: q = 'SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY uid ORDER BY ts) FROM events'
questions.sol: q = 'SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY uid ORDER BY ts) FROM events'
questions.re: ^q\s*=\s*['"]SELECT \*, ROW_NUMBER\(\) OVER \(PARTITION BY uid ORDER BY ts\) FROM events['"]$
